inputs.name: EventType
inputs.description: Type of event to which the event handler is subscribing. Must be one of the following: Alarm Event Audit Event Audit Error Event Error Event Exception Event GD End Event AGD Start Event JMS Delivery Failure Event JMS Retrieval Failure Event Journal Event Port Status Event Replication Event Security Event Session End Event Session Expire Event Session Start Event Stat Event Tx End Event Tx Start Event Tip: To view the current list of event types, you can execute the pub.event:getEventTypes service.

inputs.name: Filter
inputs.description: Selects (filters) the set of events within EventType to which the event handler is subscribing. addSubscriber uses Filter as a pattern string to filter a particular attribute of an event. The pattern string can be composed of literal characters, which match a character exactly, and/or the " * " character, which matches any sequence of characters. For example:

inputs.name: Service
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the event-handler service (the service that will execute when the event specified by EventType and Filter occurs).

inputs.name: Comment
inputs.description: Descriptive comment for this subscription. This comment is displayed when subscriptions are viewed with Designer .

inputs.name: Enabled
inputs.description: Flag specifying the status of the subscription. Must be one of the following values. Set to: true to make the subscription active. false to make the subscription inactive. This is the default. Note: Although the default value is false, you will generally want to set Enabled to true to activate the subscription immediately when it is added.

outputs.name: Result
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether the subscriber was successfully added. A value of: true indicates that the subscriber was added successfully. false indicates that the subscriber was not added.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: service
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the service that generated the event. A service can generate an alarm event when a client invokes a service that accesses information or a service on a remote server. If the client is not a member of an allowed group for the port on the remote server, the service will generate an alarm event.

inputs.name: sessionID
inputs.description: Identification number for the session during which the alarm event was generated. Some alarm events are not generated during sessions. In these cases, the sessionID variable will not contain a value.

inputs.name: msg
inputs.description: Text describing the alarm.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss z (for example, "2004-10-28 14:46:39 EDT"). Note: You can set the format for the time parameter in the watt.server.dateStampFmt property.

inputs.name: TID
inputs.description: Server thread that generated the audit event.

inputs.name: service
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: sessionID
inputs.description: Session ID of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: Description of the audit point. A value of: Started indicates that this event marks the beginning of a service. Ended indicates that this event marks the end of a service that executed successfully. Failed indicates that this event marks the end of a service that executed unsuccessfully (that is, threw an exception) and is not configured to retry. A failed event also marks the end of a service that executed unsuccessfully after exhausting all of its retries. Retried indicates that this event is created each time a service is retried.

inputs.name: pipeline
inputs.description: Optional. The pipeline that was passed to the service. This parameter is required only if the service is configured to include the pipeline when auditing.

inputs.name: userName
inputs.description: User ID that invoked the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time at which the audit error occurred. This parameter is in the format yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS (for example, "2004-10-28 14:46:39.505").

inputs.name: destination
inputs.description: Specifies the audit log destination to which the audit logging system attempted to write when the error occurred.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Error message of the exception.

inputs.name: stackTrace
inputs.description: Stack trace for the exception.

inputs.name: errorCode
inputs.description: Optional. The SQL error code returned with the exception.

outputs.name: *YourSearchTerm
outputs.description: Events that contain YourSearchTerm at the end of the audit error event value.

outputs.name: YourSearchTerm*
outputs.description: Events that contain YourSearchTerm at the beginning of the audit error event value.

outputs.name: *YourSearchTerm*
outputs.description: Events that contain YourSearchTerm anywhere within the audit error event value.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time at which the audit error occurred. This parameter is in the format yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS (for example, "2004-10-28 14:46:39.505").

inputs.name: destination
inputs.description: Specifies the audit log destination to which the audit logging system attempted to write when the error occurred.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Error message of the exception.

inputs.name: stackTrace
inputs.description: Stack trace for the exception.

inputs.name: errorCode
inputs.description: Optional. The SQL error code returned with the exception.

inputs.name: EventType
inputs.description: Type of event from which the event handler is unsubscribing. Must be one of the following values: Alarm Event Audit Event Exception Event GD End Event GD Start Event JMS Delivery Failure Event JMS Retrieval Failure Event Port Status Event Replication Event Security Event Session End Event Session Expire Event Session Start Event Stat Event Tx End Event Tx Start Event Tip: To view the current list of event types, you can execute the pub.event:getEventTypes service.

inputs.name: gID
inputs.description: ID of the subscriber that you want to delete. To get a list of subscriber IDs, execute the pub.event:getSubscribers service.

outputs.name: Result
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether the subscriber was successfully deleted. A value of: true indicates that the subscriber was deleted successfully. false indicates that the subscriber was not deleted (typically an invalid subscriber ID was provided in gID ).

inputs.name: stackTrace
inputs.description: Stack trace for the error.

inputs.name: errorMessage
inputs.description: The error message, if any, generated by the error that causes the error event.

inputs.name: serviceName
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the service in which the error occurred.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS . Note: You can set the format by specifying the watt.server.dateStampFmt property.

inputs.name: error
inputs.description: Optional. Error message of the exception.

inputs.name: localizedError
inputs.description: Optional. Error message in the language that corresponds to the locale of your webMethods installation.

inputs.name: errorType
inputs.description: Exception type that was thrown.

inputs.name: errorDump
inputs.description: More detailed information about the exception.

inputs.name: service
inputs.description: Optional. Fully qualified name of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: user
inputs.description: User that requested the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: callStack
inputs.description: Optional. The call stack information describing where the exception occurred, including the fully qualified name of a service on the stack and an index that identifies the last executed flow step in that service. Each document represents a service on the call stack. The first document in the list represents the service that threw the exception and the last document in the list represents the top-level service. The structure of this document is defined by pub.event:callstackItem .

inputs.name: pipeline
inputs.description: Optional. State of the pipeline at the time the exception occurred.

inputs.name: threadID
inputs.description: Thread ID identifying the thread that invoked the service.

inputs.name: ssnid
inputs.description: Session ID during which the exception occurred.

inputs.name: errorMsgID
inputs.description: Optional. The identification number for the error message.

inputs.name: errorDetails
inputs.description: Optional. Additional exception information provided by the author of the Java service. For more information about constructing exceptions to return additional information, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Java API Reference for the com.wm.util.LocalizedException class.

inputs.name: nestedErrorInfo
inputs.description: Optional. Nested errors and exceptions, if any. The structure of this document is defined by pub.event:exceptionInfo .

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: TID
inputs.description: Transaction ID of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: Status of the transaction.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS.

inputs.name: TID
inputs.description: Transaction ID of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: svcname
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: Status of the transaction.

inputs.name: EventTypes
inputs.description: The types of events that the server supports: Alarm Event Audit Event Audit Error Event Exception Event GD End Event GD Start Event JMS Delivery Failure Event JMS Retrieval Failure Event Port Status Event Replication Event Security Event Session End Event Session Expire Event Session Start Event Stat Event Tx End Event Tx Start Event

outputs.name: EventTypes
outputs.description: The types of events that the server supports: Alarm Event Audit Event Audit Error Event Exception Event GD End Event GD Start Event JMS Delivery Failure Event JMS Retrieval Failure Event Port Status Event Replication Event Security Event Session End Event Session Expire Event Session Start Event Stat Event Tx End Event Tx Start Event

inputs.name: EventType
inputs.description: Type of event for which you want the list of subscribers. Must be one of the following values: Alarm Event Audit Event Audit Error Event Exception Event GD End Event GD Start Event JMS Delivery Failure Event JMS Retrieval Failure Event Port Status Event Replication Event Security Event Session End Event Session Expire Event Session Start Event Stat Event Tx End Event Tx Start Event Tip: To view the current list of event types, you can execute the pub.event:getEventTypes service.

outputs.name: Subscribers
outputs.description: The list of subscribers. For each subscriber, Subscribers will contain a key that is the subscriber ID. The value of that key is a document containing the following information about the subscriber:

inputs.name: triggerName
inputs.description: Specifies the name of the JMS trigger that executed the trigger service for which the JMS retrieval failure event occurred.

inputs.name: triggerDestinationIndex
inputs.description: Specifies the index for the destination from which the JMS trigger receives messages. A JMS trigger that specifies a join type can listen for messages from multiple destinations. The first destination listed has an index of 0, the second destination listed has an indices of 1, etc.

inputs.name: deliveryCount
inputs.description: Number of times the JMS provider delivered the message to the JMS trigger at the time the event occurred.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS

inputs.name: exceptionClass
inputs.description: Name of the class that caused the failure. This may be useful to determine programmatically why the error occurred.

inputs.name: exceptionMessage
inputs.description: Message contained in the exception.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: A document (IData) containing the JMS message being processed when the error occurred.

inputs.name: aliasName
inputs.description: Name of the JMS connection alias used to send the message to the JMS provider.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Contents of the JMS message that could not be sent to the JMS provider.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/mm/dd hh:mm:ss.ss.

inputs.name: productID
inputs.description: Name of the product that generated the journal log message and the event.

inputs.name: majorCode
inputs.description: Major code of the message.

inputs.name: minorCode
inputs.description: Minor code of the message.

inputs.name: severity
inputs.description: Number indicating the severity of the message. 1 Fatal 2 Error 3 Warning 4 Info 5, 6 Debut 7 - 10 Trace

inputs.name: defaultMessage
inputs.description: Default message associated with the major code and minor code. The actual message may differ from the default message. For example the default message might be “Package WmART is stopping due to {0}”, where {0} is a placeholder for a run-time parameter. The actual message generated at run time might be “Package WmART is stopping due to ServiceException”.

inputs.name: EventType
inputs.description: Event type that you want the subscription to have: Alarm Event Audit Event Error Event Exception Event GD End Event GD Start Event JMS Delivery Failure Event JMS Retrieval Failure Event Journal Event Port Status Event Replication Event Security Event Session End Event Session Expire Event Session Start Event Stat Event Tx End Event Tx Start Event Tip: To view the current list of event types, you can execute the pub.event:getEventTypes service.

inputs.name: gID
inputs.description: ID of the subscriber that you want to modify. To get the current list of subscriber IDs, execute the pub.event:getSubscribers service.

inputs.name: Filter
inputs.description: Filter that you want subscription to have. Filter is a pattern-matching string composed of literal characters, which match a character exactly, and/or the " * " character, which matches any sequence of characters. For example:

inputs.name: Service
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the event-handler service that you want the subscription to specify.

inputs.name: Comment
inputs.description: Descriptive comment that you want to assign to the subscription.

inputs.name: Enabled
inputs.description: Flag specifying the status of the subscription. Must be one of the following values. Set to: true to make the subscription active. false to make the subscription inactive. This is the default. Note: Although the default value is false, you will generally want to set Enabled to true to activate the subscription immediately after it is modified.

outputs.name: Result
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether the subscriber was successfully modified. A value of: true indicates that the subscriber was updated successfully. false indicates that the subscriber was not updated (typically because an invalid subscriber ID was provided in gID ).

inputs.name: portStatusInfo
inputs.description: of documents (Data[] objects) containing the following information for each port.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Description of the event (such as create or push). The value of action can be used to maintain separate logs for each action type.

inputs.name: package
inputs.description: Name of package being replicated.

inputs.name: service
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: clientID
inputs.description: IP address of the host from which the request originated.

inputs.name: serverID
inputs.description: IP address of the host on which Integration Server is running.

inputs.name: userName
inputs.description: User ID that initiated or performed the security event. If a value is specified for the clientApplication field, then userName identifies the OAuth resource owner that permitted the OAuth client application to initiate or perform the security event.

inputs.name: securityEventType
inputs.description: Type of security event. Some examples are: Authentication Authorization Certificates Configuration JDBC Pools Packages Passwords Ports Remote Servers Services SSL Web Services

inputs.name: clientApplication
inputs.description: User ID of the OAuth client application that initiated or performed the security event. If this field has a value, the userName field identifies the OAuth resource owner that permitted the OAuth client application to initiate or perform the security event.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: Flag indicating whether the security action completed successfully. Set to: True to indicate that the security event completed successfully. False to indicate that the security event ended because of failure.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Indicates what the security action was, irrespective of whether it was successful or unsuccessful. For example, if a user was successfully added to Integration Server , the message would say so. If the event was unsuccessful, this string would provide a reason or information about the failure, wherever possible.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: clientID
inputs.description: IP address of the host from which the request originated.

inputs.name: serverID
inputs.description: IP address of the host on which Integration Server is running.

inputs.name: userName
inputs.description: User ID that initiated or performed the security event. If a value is specified for the clientApplication field, then userName identifies the OAuth resource owner that permitted the OAuth client application to initiate or perform the security event.

inputs.name: securityEventType
inputs.description: Type of security event. Some examples are: Authentication Authorization Certificates Configuration JDBC Pools Packages Passwords Ports Remote Servers Services SSL Web Services

inputs.name: clientApplication
inputs.description: User ID of the OAuth client application that initiated or performed the security event. If this field has a value, the userName field identifies the OAuth resource owner that permitted the OAuth client application to initiate or perform the security event.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: Flag indicating whether the security action completed successfully. True indicates that the security event completed successfully. False indicates that the security event ended because of failure.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Indicates what the security action was, irrespective of whether it was successful or unsuccessful. For example, if a user was successfully added to Integration Server , the message would say so. If the event was unsuccessful, this string would provide a meaningful reason or information about the failure, wherever possible.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred, in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: sessionID
inputs.description: Session ID of the service firing the alarm.

inputs.name: rpcs
inputs.description: Number of service calls the session has performed.

inputs.name: age
inputs.description: Number of milliseconds the session existed before it ended.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred. Given in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: sessionID
inputs.description: Session ID of the service firing the alarm.

inputs.name: rpcs
inputs.description: Number of service calls the session has performed.

inputs.name: age
inputs.description: Number of milliseconds the session existed before it expired.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred. Given in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: sessionID
inputs.description: ID of the new session.

inputs.name: userid
inputs.description: User ID that the IS client or developer used to log on to the IBM webMethods Integration Server .

inputs.name: sessionName
inputs.description: Name of the new session.

inputs.name: startTime
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred. Given in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: uptime
inputs.description: Amount of time the server has been up. Given in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: totalMem
inputs.description: Total amount of used and unused storage available to the JVM, in kilobytes. For example, a value of 65535 represents 64 megabytes of storage.

inputs.name: freeMem
inputs.description: Amount of unused storage available to the Integration Server , in kilobytes. For example, a value of 65535 represents 64 megabytes of storage.

inputs.name: usedMem
inputs.description: Amount of storage used by the Integration Server , in kilobytes. For example, a value of 65535 represents 64 megabytes of storage.

inputs.name: freeMemPer
inputs.description: Percent of total memory unused.

inputs.name: usedMemPer
inputs.description: Percent of total memory used.

inputs.name: svrT
inputs.description: Number of services currently running.

inputs.name: svrTMax
inputs.description: Peak number of servers ever running concurrently.

inputs.name: sysT
inputs.description: Number of JVM threads running.

inputs.name: sysTMax
inputs.description: Peak number of threads ever running.

inputs.name: conn
inputs.description: Number of current sessions.

inputs.name: connMax
inputs.description: Peak number of concurrent sessions.

inputs.name: reqTotal
inputs.description: Cumulative total number of services processed.

inputs.name: reqAvg
inputs.description: Average duration of service.

inputs.name: newReqPM
inputs.description: New requests per minute.

inputs.name: endReqPM
inputs.description: End requests per minute.

inputs.name: errSvc
inputs.description: Number of services completed in error state.

inputs.name: svcRate
inputs.description: Number of end/start(s) per second.

inputs.name: ssnUsed
inputs.description: Number of licensed sessions currently active.

inputs.name: ssnPeak
inputs.description: Number of licensed sessions that have ever run concurrently on the server.

inputs.name: ssnMax
inputs.description: Maximum number of sessions for which the server is licensed.

inputs.name: errSys
inputs.description: Number of unknown errors.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred. Given in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: TID
inputs.description: Transaction ID of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: Status of the transaction.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Date and time that the event occurred. Given in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SS .

inputs.name: TID
inputs.description: Transaction ID of the service that generated the event.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: Status of the transaction.
